back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,

Speak to Aaron and to his sons, and they shall keep themselves apart from the holy things of the sons of Israel, and they shall not profane My holy name—those things that they consecrate to Me. I am the Lord.

Say to them: For your generations, any man who approaches from all your offspring to the holy things that the sons of Israel consecrate to the Lord, while his uncleanness is upon him—that soul shall be cut off from before Me. I am the Lord.

Any man from the offspring of Aaron who has a skin disease or a discharge—he shall not eat of the holy things until he is clean. And whoever touches anything defiled by a corpse, or a man from whom an emission of seed has gone out,
or a man who touches any crawling creature that defiles him, or a person who defiles him with any uncleanness—
the soul that touches it shall be unclean until evening, and he shall not eat of the holy things unless he has bathed his flesh in water.
And when the sun has set, he shall be clean, and afterward he may eat of the holy things, for it is his bread.
A carcass and torn flesh he shall not eat, becoming unclean by it. I am the Lord.
And they shall keep My charge, and they shall not bear sin for it and die because of it when they profane it. I am the Lord who sanctifies them.
And no outsider shall eat the holy thing; a sojourner of a priest or a hired hand shall not eat the holy thing.
But a priest, when he buys a soul as a purchase of his money—he may eat in it. And one born in his house—they may eat in his bread.
And the daughter of a priest, when she is married to an outsider, she shall not eat of the contribution of the holy things.

But the daughter of a priest, when she is a widow or divorced and has no offspring, and she returns to her father's house as in her youth—she may eat of her father's bread. But no outsider may eat in it.
And a man, when he eats a holy thing in error, he shall add a fifth to it and give the priest the holy thing.
And they shall not profane the holy things of the sons of Israel—that which they lift up to the Lord—
and so cause them to bear iniquity of guilt by their eating their holy things, for I am the Lord who sanctifies them.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,

Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel, and say to them: Any man from the house of Israel or from the foreigner in Israel who presents his offering, for any of their vows or for any of their freewill offerings that they offer to the Lord as a burnt offering,
for your acceptance—an unblemished male, from the cattle, from the sheep, or from the goats.
Anything that has a defect in it you shall not offer, for it shall not be acceptable on your behalf.

And a man, when he presents a peace offering to the Lord to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering, from the cattle or from the flock—unblemished it shall be for acceptance; no defect shall be in it.
Blind, or broken, or maimed, or with a sore, or scab, or rash—these you shall not offer to the Lord, and a fire offering you shall not place from them on the altar to the Lord.
An ox and a sheep deformed or stunted—as a freewill offering you may make it, but as a vow it shall not be accepted.
And bruised, and crushed, and torn, and cut—you shall not offer to the Lord, and in your land you shall not do this.
And from the hand of a foreigner you shall not offer the bread of your God from any of these, because their deformity is in them, a defect is in them—they shall not be accepted on your behalf.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
An ox, or a sheep, or a goat, when it is born—it shall be seven days under its mother, and from the eighth day onward it shall be acceptable as an offering of a fire offering to the Lord.
And an ox or a sheep—it and its young you shall not slaughter on one day.
And when you sacrifice a thanksgiving sacrifice to the Lord, for your acceptance you shall sacrifice.
On that day it shall be eaten; you shall not leave any of it until morning. I am the Lord.
And you shall keep My commandments and do them. I am the Lord.
And you shall not profane My holy name, and I shall be sanctified in the midst of the sons of Israel. I am the Lord who sanctifies you,
who brought you out from the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord.
